content:
# Joshua Chapter 13 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 236-237 | **Source Image:** `../../source-texts/hebrew-OT/JOS_013_page_001.jpg`

1. And Yehoshua was old, advanced in days, and YHWH said to him, "You are old, advanced in days, and the land remaining to be possessed [is] very much.

2. This [is] the land that remains: all the regions of the Pelishtim and all the Geshurites,

3. from the Shichor which [is] upon the face of Mitsrayim, and as far as the border of Eqron northward — to the Kenaanites it is reckoned — five lords of the Pelishtim: the Azzati, and the Ashdodi, and the Ashqeloni, the Gitti, and the Eqroni; and the Avvim,

4. from the south, all the land of the Kenaanites, and Mearah which [belongs] to the Tsidonians, as far as Afeq, as far as the border of the Emori;

5. and the land of the Gevali, and all the Levanon [toward the] rising of the sun, from Baal-Gad under Mount Chermon as far as Levo-Chamat.

6. All the inhabitants of the hill country, from the Levanon as far as Misrefot-Mayim — all the Tsidonians — I will drive them out from before the sons of Yisrael. Only, cause it to fall to Yisrael as an inheritance, as I have commanded you.

7. And now, divide this land as an inheritance to the nine tribes and the half-tribe of Menasheh."

8. With it the Reuvenites and the Gadites received their inheritance which Moshe gave to them on the other side of the Yarden eastward, as Moshe, servant of YHWH, gave to them:

9. from Aroer, which [is] on the edge of the wadi Arnon, and the city which [is] in the midst of the wadi, and all the plain of Medeva as far as Divon;

10. and all the cities of Sichon, king of the Emori, who reigned in Cheshbon, as far as the border of the sons of Ammon;

11. and the Gilead, and the territory of the Geshurites and the Maakhatites, and all Mount Chermon, and all the Bashan as far as Salkhah;

12. all the kingdom of Og in the Bashan, who reigned in Ashtarot and in Edrei — he remained from the remnant of the Refaim — and Moshe struck them and drove them out.

13. And the sons of Yisrael did not drive out the Geshurites and the Maakhatites; and Geshur and Maakhat dwell in the midst of Yisrael to this day.

14. Only to the tribe of Levi he did not give an inheritance; the fire-offerings of YHWH, God of Yisrael, [are] its inheritance, as he spoke to it.

15. And Moshe gave to the tribe of the sons of Reuven according to their clans.

16. And their territory was from Aroer, which [is] on the edge of the wadi Arnon, and the city which [is] in the midst of the wadi, and all the plain by Medeva;

17. Cheshbon and all its cities which [are] in the plain: Divon, and Bamot-Baal, and Bet-Baal-Meon,

18. and Yahtsah, and Qedemot, and Mefaat,

19. and Qiryatayim, and Sivmah, and Tseret-ha-Shachar on the mountain of the valley,

20. and Bet-Peor, and the slopes of Pisgah, and Bet-ha-Yeshimot,

21. and all the cities of the plain, and all the kingdom of Sichon, king of the Emori, who reigned in Cheshbon, whom Moshe struck — him and the chiefs of Midyan: Evi, and Reqem, and Tsur, and Chur, and Reva, princes of Sichon, inhabitants of the land.

22. And Bilam son of Beor, the diviner, the sons of Yisrael killed with the sword among their slain.

23. And the border of the sons of Reuven was the Yarden and [its] border. This [was] the inheritance of the sons of Reuven according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

24. And Moshe gave to the tribe of Gad, to the sons of Gad, according to their clans.

25. And their territory was Yazer, and all the cities of the Gilead, and half the land of the sons of Ammon, as far as Aroer which [is] upon the face of Rabbah;

26. and from Cheshbon as far as Ramat-ha-Mitspeh and Betonim, and from Machanayim as far as the border of Devir;

27. and in the valley: Bet-Haram, and Bet-Nimrah, and Sukkot, and Tsafon, the rest of the kingdom of Sichon, king of Cheshbon — the Yarden and [its] border, as far as the edge of the Sea of Kinneret, on the other side of the Yarden eastward.

28. This [is] the inheritance of the sons of Gad according to their clans — the cities and their villages.

29. And Moshe gave to the half-tribe of Menasheh, and it was for the half-tribe of the sons of Menasheh according to their clans.

30. And their territory was from Machanayim: all the Bashan, all the kingdom of Og, king of the Bashan, and all the villages of Yair which [are] in the Bashan — sixty cities;

31. and half the Gilead, and Ashtarot, and Edrei, cities of the kingdom of Og in the Bashan, [were] for the sons of Makhir son of Menasheh — for half the sons of Makhir according to their clans.

32. These [are] what Moshe gave as inheritance in the plains of Moav, on the other side of the Yarden [at] Yericho, eastward.

33. And to the tribe of Levi, Moshe did not give an inheritance; YHWH, God of Yisrael — he [is] their inheritance, as he spoke to them.

---
## EMET's Textual Notes

**Manuscript and Source:** Translated from Leningrad Codex (B19A), pages 236-237, as photographed by the West Semitic Research Project (Bruce E. Zuckerman). The Hebrew text of Joshua 13 begins on page 236, continuing onto page 237. Secondary reference: Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209, folio 170), for the LXX Greek text.

**Verse 1 — וִיהוֹשֻׁעַ זָקֵן בָּא בַּיָּמִים:** The phrase בָּא בַּיָּמִים ("advanced in days," literally "come into the days") is an idiomatic expression for extreme old age. The same formula is used of Avraham (Gen 24:1) and David (1 Kgs 1:1). Rendered literally. The LXX reads Ἰησοῦς πρεσβύτερος προβεβηκὼς τῶν ἡμερῶν ("Joshua, elder, advanced in days"), confirming the Hebrew.

**Verse 3 — שִׁיחוֹר (Shichor):** The Shichor (שִׁיחוֹר, "the dark/turbid [water]") is generally identified with the easternmost branch of the Nile or the Wadi el-Arish. The Leningrad Codex reads הַשִּׁיחוֹר. The LXX renders ἀπὸ τῆς ἀοικήτου ("from the uninhabited [region]"), a significantly different reading that may reflect a different Vorlage or interpretive tradition.

**Verse 3 — סַרְנֵי (lords/tyrants):** The term סַרְנֵי פְלִשְׁתִּים uses the unique word סֶרֶן (plural סְרָנִים), a title used exclusively for Philistine rulers. It likely derives from a non-Semitic loanword, possibly related to Greek τύραννος ("tyrant") or Luwian. Rendered "lords" for clarity while preserving the distinctiveness. LXX uses ἀρχόντων (rulers).

**Verse 5 — לְבוֹא חֲמָת (Levo-Chamat):** The phrase לְבוֹא חֲמָת is debated — either "the entrance of Chamat" (a geographic marker) or a place name "Levo-Chamat" (modern Lebweh in the Beqaa valley of Lebanon). EMET preserves the transliteration since the Hebrew is ambiguous. LXX reads Λαβωμαθ, suggesting it was understood as a place name.

**Verse 6 — אָנֹכִי אוֹרִישֵׁם:** YHWH speaks in the first person: "I will drive them out." The Hiphil of ירשׁ (to cause to dispossess / to drive out) carries the force of divine agency in land conquest — a theological declaration, not merely military prediction.

**Verse 12 — יֶתֶר הָרְפָאִים (remnant of the Refaim):** Og is described as remaining מִיֶּתֶר הָרְפָאִים, "from the remnant of the Refaim." The Refaim (רְפָאִים) are the legendary giant inhabitants of the Transjordan. The term also appears in Deuteronomy 3:11 where Og's iron bedstead is described. LXX renders γιγάντων (giants).

**Verse 13 — לֹא הוֹרִישׁוּ:** "Did not drive out" — the failure to dispossess the Geshurites and Maakhatites is a recurring theme in Judges 1 and throughout Joshua 13-19. The phrase וַיֵּשֶׁב... בְּקֶרֶב יִשְׂרָאֵל עַד הַיּוֹם הַזֶּה ("and [they] dwell in the midst of Yisrael to this day") is a formulaic editorial marker indicating composition or redaction well after the settlement period.

**Verse 14 — אִשֵּׁי יהוה (fire-offerings of YHWH):** The Leningrad Codex reads אִשֵּׁי יְהוָה, "the fire-offerings of YHWH" — that is, the priestly portions from sacrifices constitute Levi's inheritance. The LXX has a different tradition, reading κύριος ὁ θεὸς Ισραηλ, οὗτος κληρονομία αὐτῶν ("the Lord God of Israel, he [is] their inheritance"), which aligns more closely with verse 33. Some scholars suggest the MT of v.14 preserves an older tradition distinguishing between the material inheritance (sacrificial portions) and the theological statement of v.33 (YHWH himself).

**Verse 21 — נְשִׂיאֵי סִיחוֹן (princes of Sichon):** The five Midyanite chiefs — Evi, Reqem, Tsur, Chur, and Reva — are called both נְסִיכֵי ("vassals/installed ones") and נְשִׂיאֵי סִיחוֹן ("princes of Sichon"), indicating they were subordinate kings or chieftains under Sichon's authority. Their death is recounted in Numbers 31:8.

**Verse 22 — בִּלְעָם בֶּן-בְּעוֹר הַקּוֹסֵם:** Bilam is called הַקּוֹסֵם ("the diviner"), a derogatory designation contrasting with his designation as a prophet-figure in Numbers 22-24. The term קוֹסֵם (qosem) in Deuteronomy 18:10 is explicitly forbidden in Israel. His execution בַּחֶרֶב ("with the sword") is noted alongside the Midyanite slain (cf. Num 31:8).

**Verse 27 — יָם כִּנֶּרֶת (Sea of Kinneret):** The Leningrad Codex reads יָם כִּנֶּרֶת, the Sea of Kinneret (later known as the Sea of Galilee / Lake Gennesaret). The name likely derives from the harp-like shape of the lake (כִּנּוֹר = harp). LXX reads τῆς θαλάσσης Χενερεθ.

**Verse 33 — YHWH as inheritance:** The chapter closes with a restatement of Levi's unique status: יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵי יִשְׂרָאֵל הוּא נַחֲלָתָם — "YHWH, God of Yisrael, he [is] their inheritance." This forms an inclusio with verse 14, framing the Transjordan allotments between two declarations of Levi's exclusion from territorial inheritance. The emphatic pronoun הוּא ("he") underscores the theological point that YHWH himself — not land — is Levi's portion.
